tr.td: "Im sick" [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 11:39:49 AM]

section.title: MEDICAL EQUIPMENT
section.text: Patient has no history of implanted devices.

tr.td: Theraflu Warming Severe Cold Daytime oral syrup

Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily

Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily

tr.td: Infectious agent antigen detection by immunoassay technique, (eg, enzyme immunoassay [EIA], enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay [ELISA], immunochemiluminometric assay [IMCA]) qualitative or semiquantitative, multiple-step method;severe acitye respiratory syndrome coronavirus [...SARS-COV-2]
Infectious agent antigen detection by immunoassay with direct optical observation; Influenza
Demonstration and/or evaluation of patient utilization of an aerosol generator, nebulizer, metered dose inhaler or IPPB device

Office or other outpatient visit for the evaluation and management of an established patient, which requires at least 2 of these 3 key components: A detailed history; A detailed examination; Medical decision making of moderate complexity. Counseling and/or coordination of care with other physicians, other qualified health care professionals, or agencies are provided consistent with the nature of the problem(s) and the patient's and/or family's needs. Usually, the presenting problem(s) are of moderate to high severity. Typically, 25 minutes are spent face-to-face with the patient and/or family.
Most recent systolic blood pressure < 140mm Hg Carrier judgment
Most recent diastolic blood pressure < 90mm Hg Carrier judgment
BMI is documented within normal parameters and no follow-up plan is required Carrier judgment
Eligible professional attests to documenting in the medical record they obtained, updated, or reviewed the patient's current medications Carrier judgment
Current tobacco non-user (CAD, CAP, COPD, PV) (DM) (IBD)
Influenza immunization administered or previously given M1299
Electrocardiogram, routine ECG with at least 12 leads; with interpretation and report

Infectious agent antigen detection by immunoassay technique, (eg, enzyme immunoassay [EIA], enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay [ELISA], immunochemiluminometric assay [IMCA]) qualitative or semiquantitative, multiple-step method;severe acitye respiratory syndrome coronavirus [...SARS-COV-2]
Infectious agent antigen detection by immunoassay with direct optical observation; Influenza
Immunization administration (includes percutaneous, intradermal, subcutaneous, or intramuscular injections); 1 vaccine (single or combination vaccine/toxoid)
Hepatitis B vaccine (HepB), adult dosage, 2 dose schedule, for intramuscular use
Electrocardiogram, routine ECG with at least 12 leads; with interpretation and report

section.title: HEALTH CONCERNS
section.text: No health concerns information is available.

section.title: ASSESSMENTS
section.text: # Right lower lobe pneumonia (J18.1):start dosxy, rest, push fluids # Acute bronchiolitis (J21.9):star albuterol with spacer, teaching provided # Hypertension (I10):advedid not to use stimulent decongestants # Hyperlipidemia due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (E11.69):pt immunocompromized # Patient immunocompromised (D84.9):

item.paragraph: Full Progress Note signed by Gretchen Lockard, MD
Chief Complaint: "Im sick" [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 11:39:49 AM]

History of Present Illness: 1) Sick Visit: - Symptoms started: 4 days ago - Myalgia (muscle pain, soreness):yes - Fatigue: yes - Fever/Chills:f- y, chills- no Tmax 101 - Cough:yes, deep, - Sputum Production: yes, yellow - Ear Pain/Discomfort: no - Nasal Congestion:y - Runny Nose:no - Sinus Pressure:n - Sore Throat:when I ough - Shortness of Breath:no - Wheezing: sometimes - Sick Contacts: wedding [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 11:39:49 AM]
Review of Systems: [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 11:39:49 AM]

Past Medical History: HTN, Hyperlipidemia, DM type2, osteoarthrisit
SURGERIES: appy chole TAH-BSO [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 11:39:49 AM]

Family History: Father: Cerebrovascular accident, Hypertension; Hyperlipidemia passed at age 58 Stroke Mother: No significant health history. [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 11:39:49 AM] ======== Structured Family History ======== Father: Cerebrovascular accident, Hypertension; History of HTN, Hyperlipidemia passed at age 58 Stroke Mother: No significant health history.
Social History: [Tobacco: Never smoker] EtOH: 1 beer a night dugs: none widowed retired teacher exercises 5 days aweek at the gym [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 11:39:49 AM]
Allergies: azithromycin, latex

Medications: 1) AMOXICILLIN 500MG, ONE PO BID 2) lisinopril 10 mg-hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 mg tablet, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 3) Robitussin Nighttime Cough DM 12.5 mg-30 mg/10 mL oral liquid 4) Theraflu Warming Severe Cold Daytime oral syrup 5) Trulicity 4.5 mg/0.5 mL subcutaneous pen injector, 1 injection Subq once a week

Physical Examination: - General: fatigued, but nontoxic appearing, NAD - HEENT: sclera white. TM's normal appearing. Nasal turbinates mormal Posterior pharynx no erythema, no exudate. no sinus TTP -Neck: No significant adenopathy - CV: regular rhythm, regular rate, no murmurs - Resp: thorax symmetric with good excursion, diffusely wheeze bilaterally, RLL crqackles - Neuro: alert, awake, normal speech Flu and covid today: neg/neg CXR : clear [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 11:39:49 AM]

Assessment Plan: # Right lower lobe pneumonia (J18.1):start dosxy, rest, push fluids # Acute bronchiolitis (J21.9):star albuterol with spacer, teaching provided # Hypertension (I10):advedid not to use stimulent decongestants # Hyperlipidemia due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (E11.69):pt immunocompromized # Patient immunocompromised (D84.9):
Full Progress Note signed by Raymond Decker, MD
Chief Complaint: Complete Physical
History of Present Illness: PE CXR EKG ETT BMD COLONOSCOPY Td FLU PNEUMOVAX ZOSTERVAX COVID

Review of Systems: GEN: No fevers, chills, weight loss or gain that was not intentional. EYES: No redness, discharge, pain, or changes in vision. ENT: No tinnitus, hearing loss, nasal discharge, sore throat, or swollen glands. CARDIO: No chest pain, palpitations, or pedal edema. RESP: No shortness of breath or productive cough. GI: No nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, melena, or hematochezia. GU: No burning on urination, frequency, or discharge. M/S: No joint pain or swelling. DERM: Denies pruritus rash or suspicious lesions. NEURO: Denies confusion, altered mental status, syncope, or dizziness. PSYCH: Denies anxiety, depression, or panic attacks HEM/LYMPH: Denies bleeding or bruises. Denies swollen lymph nodes.

Family History: ======== Structured Family History ======== Father: Cerebrovascular accident, Hypertension; History of HTN, Hyperlipidemia passed at age 58 Stroke Mother: No significant health history.
Allergies: azithromycin, latex

Medications: 1) AMOXICILLIN 500MG, ONE PO BID 2) lisinopril 10 mg-hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 mg tablet, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 3) Robitussin Nighttime Cough DM 12.5 mg-30 mg/10 mL oral liquid 4) Theraflu Warming Severe Cold Daytime oral syrup 5) Trulicity 4.5 mg/0.5 mL subcutaneous pen injector, 1 injection Subq once a week

Physical Examination: GEN: wdwn appears stated age. HEENT: NCAT, TM clear with normal external canals, PERRLA EOMI without nystagmus. Normal naso/oropharynx. Neg LAD. Neg B/T/TM. NECK: Supple. No bruits noted. Thyroid size WNL, no masses. RESP: CTA Bilaterally. CARDIO: RRR /s M/G/R Normal palpation. ABD: S/NT/ND/+BS. Neg HSM. No guarding or rebound. Normal percussion. GU: Penis without lesions. Scrotum without testicular masses. No hernias noted. Prostate smooth and nonenlarged. No nodularity noted. Ifob/ hemoccult negative. M/S: FROM /s crepitus. Spine nontender. Gait smooth and even. BACK: No CVA tenderness. NEURO: A & O x 3. Mtr 5+ Reflexes 2+ Sensory grossly intact. PSYCH: Judgement and insight intact. Mood normal. Does not appear anxious or depressed. EXTREMITIES: No edema. DERM: Generally Intact without lesions noted.
Labs Ordered
CBC UA EKG CXR
CMP XOL TSH FT4 TEST FTEST PRLTN
PSA
ESR VIT D
IFOB
Assessment Plan: # Hypertension (I10): Continue current meds # Familial hypercholesterolemia, unspecified (E78.019):
Full Progress Note signed by Autumn Mejia, PA-C
Allergies: azithromycin, latex
Medications: 1) Physical Therapy to Eval and treat 2) Robitussin Nighttime Cough DM 12.5 mg-30 mg/10 mL oral liquid 3) Theraflu Severe Cold and Cough 5 mg-10 mg-325 mg/15 mL oral liquid
(Encounter was coded as: Medium Complexity >)
